assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: The response does not mention checking for deprecated API usage or API version changes, which is important when upgrading Kubernetes versions

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: The response specifically covers admission webhook compatibility issues with commands to check webhook configurations and failures

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: The response addresses resource pressure from new system components and includes commands to check node resource usage and allocated resources

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: The response includes specific commands to check system pod health and look for crashlooping pods in kube-system namespace

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: The response includes commands to check API server metrics including 'kubectl get --raw /metrics' for apiserver_request_duration_seconds and request totals

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: The response addresses service mesh issues with commands to check Istio/Anthos Service Mesh pods and logs, though NetworkPolicy is not explicitly mentioned

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: The response includes commands to check events and specifically looks for evicted pods and resource pressure that could cause scheduling issues

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: The response does not mention checking HPA (Horizontal Pod Autoscaler) or VPA (Vertical Pod Autoscaler) behavior changes after the upgrade
